subject: literacy
title: Grade 3 Literacy
description: Paragraphs, story elements, nouns, verbs, and adjectives.

lessons.title: Story Elements
lessons.objective: Identify the main parts of a story: characters, setting, and plot.
lessons.content:
Every good story has these KEY PARTS:

👤 CHARACTERS — the people or animals in the story
  Main character: the most important one
  Supporting characters: helpers, friends, or villains

🏡 SETTING — WHERE and WHEN the story takes place
  Where: a forest, a school, a castle, outer space
  When: long ago, present day, the future

📖 PLOT — WHAT HAPPENS in the story
  BEGINNING — introduces characters and setting
  MIDDLE — the PROBLEM or challenge appears
  END — the problem is SOLVED (resolution)

Example: Goldilocks and the Three Bears
  Characters: Goldilocks, three bears
  Setting: a cottage in the forest
  Problem: Goldilocks uses the bears' things without asking
  Resolution: The bears come home and Goldilocks runs away!

exercises.question: What does the 'setting' of a story tell us?
options: Where and when the story happens
exercises.correctAnswer: Where and when the story happens
exercises.hint: Think about the place and time of the story.
exercises.explanation: The setting tells us WHERE the story takes place (a forest, a school) and WHEN it happens (past, present, future).

exercises.question: In a story plot, the 'middle' usually introduces what?
exercises.hint: The beginning introduces characters and setting, the middle has the exciting part...
exercises.explanation: The middle of a story usually introduces the problem or challenge that the characters need to solve!

lessons.title: Nouns, Verbs, and Adjectives
lessons.objective: Identify and use nouns, verbs, and adjectives correctly.
lessons.content:
Words have different JOBS in a sentence:

📦 NOUNS — naming words (people, places, things)
  cat, London, happiness, teacher, park
  Ask: 'Can I put THE in front of it?' → The cat ✅

🏃 VERBS — doing or being words (actions)
  run, jump, eat, is, was, think
  Ask: 'Can I do it?' or 'Can I be it?'

🎨 ADJECTIVES — describing words (tell us more about nouns)
  big, happy, red, fluffy, old
  Ask: 'What is it LIKE?'

✏️ Example: The fluffy cat sat on the old mat.
  Nouns: cat, mat
  Verb: sat
  Adjectives: fluffy, old

exercises.question: In the sentence 'The happy dog ran fast', which word is the adjective?
exercises.hint: An adjective describes a noun — which word tells us what the dog is like?
exercises.explanation: 'Happy' is the adjective — it describes the dog, telling us what the dog is like!

exercises.question: In 'The cat jumped over the fence', which word is the verb? (one word)
exercises.hint: The verb is the 'doing' word — what action did the cat do?
exercises.explanation: 'Jumped' is the verb — it tells us what the cat DID. Verbs are action words!
